name: Eunomia
description: Eunomia, in Greek mythology, is one of the Horae (Hours), goddesses of order, justice, and law in cities. She is the daughter of Zeus and Themis, the Titan goddess of divine law and order. Her sisters are Dike (Justice) and Eirene (Peace), and together they represent the stability and well-being of a well-ordered society. Eunomia specifically embodies good order, lawful conduct, and the enforcement of just laws. She ensures that communities adhere to established norms and principles, fostering harmony and preventing chaos. As a deity, Eunomia lacks a prominent individual mythology, but her significance lies in her role as a personification of essential societal values. She is often depicted as a beautiful woman holding scales or a scepter, symbols of justice and authority. Her influence is felt in the proper functioning of government and the maintenance of social equilibrium. Eunomia's presence guarantees a society that is fair, predictable, and prosperous. While she may not be as widely known as other Olympian gods, Eunomia's importance in upholding the foundations of civilization is undeniable. She represents the ideal of a society governed by reason, law, and a commitment to justice, ensuring the well-being and prosperity of its citizens. Without Eunomia, society would descend into anarchy and disorder.
subtitle: Goddess of Good Order and Law

keyMyths.title: Birth of Eunomia
keyMyths.description: Eunomia, along with her sisters, Dike and Eirene, was born from the union of Zeus and Themis. They were destined to uphold order and justice in the world.

keyMyths.title: Guardians of Olympus
keyMyths.description: As Horae, Eunomia and her sisters guarded the gates of Olympus. They ensured only those worthy could enter the realm of the gods.

keyMyths.title: Ensuring Social Order
keyMyths.description: Eunomia's presence ensured that cities were governed by just laws and that citizens adhered to established norms, preventing chaos and promoting harmony.

sources.author: Hesiod
sources.description: A major source for Greek mythology, detailing the genealogy of the gods.
sources.text: A major source for Greek mythology, detailing the genealogy of the gods.

sources.author: Homeric Hymns
